t: Authenticating users with Sign in with Apple
a: Securely authenticate users and create accounts for them in your app.

t: Bringing new apps and users into your team
a: Receive Sign in with Apple users and associated apps from another team.

t: Communicating using the private email relay service
a: Stay in touch with users who opt for an anonymous email address with Sign in with Apple.

t: Configuring your environment for Sign in with Apple
a: Authenticate users with your web service by associating an existing app with a Services ID and private key.

t: Configuring your webpage for Sign in with Apple
a: Prepare your webpage to authorize users through Sign in with Apple.

t: Displaying Sign in with Apple buttons in your app
a: Configure the appearance of Sign in with Apple buttons programatically.

t: Displaying Sign in with Apple buttons on the web
a: Configure the appearance of Sign in with Apple buttons with CSS styles.

t: Incorporating Sign in with Apple into other platforms
a: Add Sign in with Apple capabilities to apps that can’t directly access Sign in with Apple JS.

t: Processing changes for Sign in with Apple accounts
a: Manage user-initiated modifications to maintain privacy with server-to-server notifications.

t: Transferring your apps and users to another team
a: Migrate Sign in with Apple users to another team.
